channel.title: World
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from World, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Saint Francis of Assisi’s skeleton goes on public display for first time
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of visitors expected for month-long display of remains of 13th-century saint Saint Francis of Assisi’s skeleton is going on full...

item.title: ‘One of the legends’: Bad Bunny joins tributes to US salsa pioneer Willie Colón
item.description: Puerto Rican rapper speaks at concert of Colón’s influence after trombonist, vocalist and composer dies aged 75 Tributes have poured in from stars inc...

item.title: Greenland does not need US hospital boat sent by Trump, says Denmark
item.description: Defence minister rebuffs US president’s claim that Arctic islanders are ‘not being taken care of’ Greenland does not need medical assistance from othe...

item.title: Ministers lay out plans to reduce gap between poorest and most affluent pupils - UK politics live
item.description: The government plans to halve the attainment gap in England The government is considering introducing legislation to remove Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor...

item.title: Iran willing to dilute uranium stockpile as fresh protests erupt
item.description: Proposal will be at heart of offer to US as Trump considers whether to attack Iran Iran is refusing to export its 300kg stockpile of highly enriched u...

item.title: Your guide to the final day
item.description: What's happening and who to look out for at the 2026 Winter Olympics in Milan-Cortina.

item.title: Trump says he will increase his new global tariffs to 15%
item.description: After most of his tariffs were outlawed on Friday, Trump announced new global tariffs of 10% - which he says he has now increased to 15%.

item.title: Pakistan launches deadly strikes on Afghanistan
item.description: Pakistan says the strikes were retaliation for recent suicide bombings in the country.

item.title: 'Effective' SEND support won't be taken away, minister says
item.description: But the education secretary says children "will be reviewed in terms of their needs", under planned reforms.

item.title: Is Borthwick under pressure after nightmare defeat?
item.description: "Everyone has to take a look at themselves" - what went wrong for England in their record Six Nations home defeat by Ireland?
